Content: Cameraman: How are we going to continue making this documentary without spoiling anything?
Content_zh: Cameraman：我們要怎麼繼續拍這部紀錄片，才不會爆雷？

Content: Schafer: I don't know.
Content_zh: Schafer：我不知道。

Content: People know now if they've, hopefully they're not watching this episode before they finish Act One.
Content_zh: 如果大家已經知道了……希望他們不是還沒玩完第一幕就來看這集。

Content: You really shouldn't watch this episode.
Content_zh: 你真的不該看這集。

Content: Should we you shoot that now?
Content_zh: 要現在拍那段嗎？

Content: Hi. I'm Tim Schafer.
Content_zh: 嗨，我是 Tim Schafer。

Content: Please don't watch this episode until you finish Act One of Broken Age.
Content_zh: 請先玩完《Broken Age》第一幕，再來看這集。

Content: Offscreen Voice: Backers that play it, or are you more nervous about the money,
Content_zh: 畫外音：玩過的贊助者，還是你比較擔心錢，

Content: like how much it's going to sell?
Content_zh: 像是它會賣多少？

Content: Schafer: No. I mean, I feel like it's tense about the money because we want it to do well, because I have big plans for Act Two.
Content_zh: Schafer：不。我是說，我覺得錢這件事很緊張，因為我們希望它賣得好，因為我對第二幕有很大的計畫。

Content: And if we don't do well, Justin will be like, look, look, we can't, we got to, you know.
Content_zh: 如果賣不好，Justin 就會說：「你看、你看，我們不能，我們得，你知道。」

Content: Because basically, just spending our own money from here on out.
Content_zh: 因為基本上，從現在開始我們都是在花自己的錢。

Content: And probably like a few months back.
Content_zh: 而且大概幾個月前就開始了。

Content: So if no one buys the game tomorrow, maybe Act Two will be a much smaller than Act One.
Content_zh: 所以如果明天沒有人買這款遊戲，也許第二幕就會比第一幕小很多。

Content: I hope one person buys it.
Content_zh: 我希望至少有一個人買。

Content: If no one buys it, it's going to be really awkward.
Content_zh: 如果沒人買，那會很尷尬。

Content: But I guess that's a good clear, I can say that to the backers.
Content_zh: 不過我想這倒是很清楚，我可以跟贊助者這麼說。

Content: Look, nobody bought it, except for you guys.
Content_zh: 你看，除了你們以外，沒有人買。

Content: I'm sorry.
Content_zh: 抱歉。

Content: But it serves me right for investing our own money in a game.
Content_zh: 但誰叫我活該，拿自己的錢投資遊戲。

Content: I should have just played with their money.
Content_zh: 我應該只拿他們的錢來玩的。

Content: [Music] Schafer: We are launching Red's tomorrow.
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：我們明天要推出 Red’s。

Content: Crazy times.
Content_zh: 真是瘋狂的時候。

Content: All of our backers.
Content_zh: 我們所有的贊助者

Content: Thank you to our team for working so hard.
Content_zh: 謝謝我們的團隊這麼努力。

Content: We are going to the backers tomorrow and the rest of the general population, future backers, if you will on the 28th.
Content_zh: 明天要開放給贊助者，然後 28 號開放給其他一般大眾，也就是未來的贊助者。

Content: Rice: The game is up.
Content_zh: Rice：遊戲上架了。

Content: Preorders are up.
Content_zh: 預購也上線了。

Content: Schafer: Whoohoo! Rice: Yeah.
Content_zh: Schafer：嗚呼！Rice：對。

Content: Schafer: All right, next game.
Content_zh: Schafer：好，下一款遊戲。

Content: Rice: I will send the press release.
Content_zh: Rice：我會寄新聞稿。

Content: One of the reviewers said he didn't go to bed.
Content_zh: 有一位評測者說他沒去睡覺。

Content: Finished the game last night.
Content_zh: 昨晚玩完了

Content: Remo: Finished it last night?
Content_zh: Remo：昨晚玩完了？

Content: Rice: Yeah. Remo: Wow.
Content_zh: Rice：對。Remo：哇。

Content: Rice: Yeah, I sent the codes at like...
Content_zh: Rice：對，我是在大概……寄出序號的。

Content: Remo: Did he say how long it took him?
Content_zh: Remo：他說他花了多久？

Content: Rice: 4:00 or 5:00 p.m. Remo: Oh, right.
Content_zh: Rice：下午 4 點或 5 點。Remo：喔，對。

Content: Rice: So I mean, I guess there's time.
Content_zh: Rice：所以我是說，我想時間是夠的。

Content: But yeah, he just didn't stop.
Content_zh: 不過對，他就是一直沒停。

Content: That's kind of awesome.
Content_zh: 這還滿棒的。

Content: Schafer: Man, this is weird.
Content_zh: Schafer：老天，這感覺好怪。

Content: People are playing Broken Age.
Content_zh: 人們在玩《Broken Age》

Content: Seven people watching him and I'm one of them.
Content_zh: 有七個人在看他，而我是其中一個。

Content: I don't want them to know this is what I do.
Content_zh: 我不想讓他們知道我都在做這種事。

Content: I've never done this before.
Content_zh: 我從沒做過這種事

Content: We've never been able to do this before and have live streams, like after Grim Fandango.
Content_zh: 以前我們從來沒辦法這樣，像《Grim Fandango》之後那時候也沒有直播。

Content: Remo: People are already posting screenshots.
Content_zh: Remo：大家已經在貼截圖了。

Content: Bailey: I'm still waiting.
Content_zh: Bailey：我還在等。

Content: I'm just curious who's going to be the first one to like post a review.
Content_zh: 我只是好奇誰會第一個發評測。

Content: Remo: Yeah, right.
Content_zh: Remo：是啊

Content: Rice: Tim, did you hear that? Schafer: What?
Content_zh: Rice：Tim，你聽到了嗎？Schafer：什麼？

Content: Rice: Digital Trends posted an article, "Why We're Breaking the Broken Age Embargo." And how they're going to review it.
Content_zh: Rice：Digital Trends 發了一篇文章，〈為什麼我們要打破《Broken Age》的禁評期〉，還有他們打算怎麼評測。

Content: Schafer: Well, what embargo are they breaking?
Content_zh: Schafer：呃，他們要打破什麼禁評期？

Content: Rice: The review embargo.
Content_zh: Rice：評測禁評期。

Content: They're going to put a review up.
Content_zh: 他們要發評測。

Content: Schafer: They're just announcing they're going to break it?
Content_zh: Schafer：他們只是宣布自己要打破禁評期？

Content: Rice: Yeah. Schafer: Why?
Content_zh: Rice：對。Schafer：為什麼？

Content: Rice: Because they paid for the game and they're a backer.
Content_zh: Rice：因為他們付錢買了遊戲，而且他們是贊助者。

Content: And they're getting it because they paid for it.
Content_zh: 他們會拿到遊戲，是因為他們付了錢。

Content: Schafer: But we, but they're not, they're in the Beta...
Content_zh: Schafer：可是我們，可是他們不是，他們在 Beta……

Content: Rice: They haven't reviewed it yet, but Digital Trends, I know.
Content_zh: Rice：他們還沒有評測，不過 Digital Trends，我知道。

Content: They emailed me about this yesterday and that's what I told them.
Content_zh: 他們昨天有為這件事寄信給我，我也是這樣跟他們說的。

Content: Remo: Technically no one signed anything so there's nothing we can actually do about it now. Rice: Yeah.
Content_zh: Remo：嚴格來說沒有人簽任何東西，所以我們現在其實也不能拿他怎樣。Rice：對。

Content: Schafer: What do you think about that? Bailey: I'm sorry?
Content_zh: Schafer：你覺得呢？Bailey：不好意思？

Content: Rice: Yeah, I don't think there's anything we can about it.
Content_zh: Rice：對，我覺得我們也不能拿這件事怎樣。

Content: Bailey: Well, unless we have something in writing.
Content_zh: Bailey：除非我們有白紙黑字的東西。

Content: Remo: No, we don't. Embargoes are not really enforceable.
Content_zh: Remo：沒有，我們沒有。禁評期其實沒有什麼強制力。

Content: Bailey: Exactly. It's a professional...
Content_zh: Bailey：沒錯。這是專業上的……

Content: Remo: I know, I know. It's a courtesy.
Content_zh: Remo：我知道，我知道。這是一種禮貌。

Content: A professional courtesy, yeah.
Content_zh: 一種專業禮貌，對。

Content: Schafer: Well, maybe he didn't agree to anything but he knows he's being a shit.
Content_zh: Schafer：嗯，也許他沒有同意任何事，但他知道自己是在耍混蛋。

Content: Schafer: It's like people don't realize that everything's embargo.
Content_zh: Schafer：大家好像沒有意識到，每篇評測都有禁評期。

Content: Like every review they read has usually been embargoed, because your game's coming out and people want the press for the game
Content_zh: 他們讀到的每篇評測通常都有禁評期，因為你的遊戲要上市了，大家希望遊戲的媒體報導

Content: to come up on launch day or around that time people can buy it.
Content_zh: 能在發行日或大家差不多能買到的時候出來。

Content: 'Cause if it comes out before, then all that energy, people see that article, they can't go buy it.
Content_zh: 因為如果提早出來，那股聲勢，大家看到那篇文章，卻不能去買。

Content: So anyway.
Content_zh: 總之

Content: You want your, you want to time your press to that day, but different reporters can come in at different times
Content_zh: 你會想把媒體報導安排在那一天，但不同記者會在不同時間加入，

Content: and they're like hey, I want to, you know, just because this guy came in a week early,
Content_zh: 他們會說，嘿，我想，你知道，就因為這傢伙早一週進來，

Content: does that mean his review gets to go up a week earlier?
Content_zh: 就代表他的評測可以早一週發嗎？

Content: So you say it's embargoed, so that all the press has a fair chance of getting their story, not getting scooped or whatever.
Content_zh: 所以你說有禁評期，讓所有媒體都有公平機會寫自己的報導，不會被別人搶先之類的。

Content: And they also have time with the build to do a thoughtful build instead of racing to like getting it out early.
Content_zh: 而且他們也有時間用這個版本好好寫評測，而不是急著搶先發出去。

Content: But we were going out to backers and 90,000 people had the build and I think most of the regular backers were like,
Content_zh: 但我們開放給贊助者時，有 9 萬人拿到版本，我想大多數一般贊助者都會覺得，

Content: OK, yeah, this is private information.
Content_zh: 好，對，這是私人資訊。

Content: I won't spoil it for people who can't buy it yet.
Content_zh: 我不會爆雷給還不能買的人。

Content: And the press understood it.
Content_zh: 媒體也明白

Content: But there's kind of this grey area of people between backers and press, like a, kind of like a blogger, you know, who's a backer,
Content_zh: 但贊助者和媒體之間有一塊灰色地帶，像是，有點像部落客，你知道，他也是贊助者，

Content: who's like, hey, I paid money for this.
Content_zh: 他會說，嘿，我可是付了錢的。

Content: This guy's amazed to see consumers suggest the press should stick their heads in the sand because Double Fine asked them to nicely.
Content_zh: 這個人很驚訝看到消費者建議媒體，因為 Double Fine 好聲好氣地拜託，就該把頭埋進沙子裡。

Content: For the evening crowd, the Broken Age embargo's a bit of a joke.
Content_zh: 對晚間讀者來說，《Broken Age》的禁評期有點像笑話。

Content: Has anyone else contacted you from actual news outlet? Rice: IGN.
Content_zh: 還有其他真正新聞媒體的人聯絡你嗎？Rice：IGN。

Content: Schafer: Do they want to review it or just, what'd they say?
Content_zh: Schafer：他們想評測，還是只是，他們怎麼說？

Content: Rice: Yeah, it was just yesterday.
Content_zh: Rice：對，就是昨天的事。

Content: It's like I just want to be clear on what's expected.
Content_zh: 大概是說，我只是想弄清楚你們期待我們怎麼做。

Content: He says like I feel like there's so many people that are going to be having the game.
Content_zh: 他說，我覺得會有非常多人拿到遊戲。

Content: Like if other people start running their reviews, we're going to feel like we're going to have to run ours.
Content_zh: 如果其他人開始發評測，我們會覺得自己也得發。

Content: That's basically what they said.
Content_zh: 基本上就是這麼說的

Content: Schafer: Why don't we just let the people who are not being jerks about it break the embargo?
Content_zh: Schafer：我們為什麼不乾脆讓那些沒有耍混蛋的人打破禁評期？

Content: Rice: We can't do that.
Content_zh: Rice：我們不能這麼做

Content: Schafer: And that would scoop all the people who are being jerks about it.
Content_zh: Schafer：這樣就能搶先那些耍混蛋的人。

Content: Why, we can't do that?
Content_zh: 為什麼，我們不能這麼做？

Content: Rice: I don't know how to enforce that.
Content_zh: Rice：我不知道要怎麼執行。

Content: Schafer: Just pick the ones that are being nice about it and just tell them they can.
Content_zh: Schafer：就挑那些態度好的，告訴他們可以發。

Content: Then one day later tell everyone else.
Content_zh: 然後晚一天再告訴其他人。

Content: Rice: Yeah. I think that only serves to inflate the issue, because then they think the embargo is still there
Content_zh: Rice：對。我覺得那只會讓問題變得更大，因為他們會以為禁評期還在，

Content: and then it's being broken and then it's a mess.
Content_zh: 然後有人打破禁評期，接著就一團亂。

Content: So I think we have to, Schafer: It's kind of a mess, though, I think now.
Content_zh: 所以我覺得我們得，Schafer：不過我覺得現在已經有點亂了。

Content: Rice: Well, then, that's the case and I think we have to talk about lifting it.
Content_zh: Rice：嗯，如果是這樣，我想我們得討論解除禁評期。

Content: And then I'm fine like, I've been saving the list of people I've emailed all the review stuff
Content_zh: 那我也沒問題，我一直有留著我寄過所有評測資料的名單，

Content: so it would really be like a two second Kickstarter update and emailing them all, if you want to just totally lift things.
Content_zh: 所以如果你想完全解除，其實就是發一則超短的 Kickstarter 更新，然後寄信給所有人。

Content: Schafer: Maybe we could just open it in a couple days, kind of.
Content_zh: Schafer：也許我們可以幾天後再開放，大概吧。

Content: Rice: Just sit on it for a second and see.
Content_zh: Rice：先觀望一下再說。

Content: Schafer: Yeah. Rice: OK.
Content_zh: Schafer：對。Rice：好。

Content: Schafer: God, that embargo's a disaster.
Content_zh: Schafer：天啊，那個禁評期真是一場災難。

Content: Schafer: And so because like one or two people were threatening to like break it, that all of a sudden made everybody else who wasn't breaking it like a chump.
Content_zh: Schafer：因為一兩個人威脅要打破禁評期，突然讓其他沒有打破的人都像傻子一樣。

Content: And I didn't want them, you know, to feel, so we just like, look, OK.
Content_zh: 我不想讓他們，你知道，覺得那樣，所以我們就說，好吧。

Content: We take back our request that no one's, go ahead.
Content_zh: 我們收回不讓任何人發的請求，發吧。

Content: Post all the reviews and stuff.
Content_zh: 把所有評測之類的都發出來。

Content: So a bunch of our reviews went up when people couldn't buy the game.
Content_zh: 所以在人們還不能買遊戲的時候，我們一堆評測就上線了。

Content: So that was kind, it took away a lot of our, like our press hit for the launch day, which is too bad.
Content_zh: 所以那等於拿走了我們很多發行日的媒體聲量，這很可惜。

Content: But it seemed like it was, it would, we just couldn't, it was out of control.
Content_zh: 但當時感覺就是，我們真的沒辦法，事情已經失控了。

Content: There was just like 90,000 people, even if like 80,000 of them are totally like, yeah, it's cool.
Content_zh: 畢竟有 9 萬人，就算其中 8 萬人都完全覺得，對，沒問題。

Content: But if like just a few took one or two breaks, then the whole thing just falls apart.
Content_zh: 但只要少數幾個人破例一兩次，整件事就會垮掉。

Content: Schafer: All our backers are so cool, but the backers who are in the press feel a little differently.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們的贊助者都很酷，但身為媒體的贊助者感受有點不同。

Content: They feel they have the option to not be cool.
Content_zh: 他們覺得自己可以選擇不要那麼酷。

Content: It's an awkward situation.
Content_zh: 情況很尷尬

Content: Next time we wouldn't, I think actually have private forums for that reason.
Content_zh: 下次我們不會這樣，我其實覺得這就是需要私人論壇的原因。

Content: Schafer: I mean, I feel in end that I just don't, I just don't want to have this fight any more about private stuff and all.
Content_zh: Schafer：我是說，最後我覺得我就是不想再為了私人內容之類的事吵下去了。

Content: It just puts, I mean, being in this antagonist relationship with the press is just not comfortable,
Content_zh: 我的意思是，跟媒體處在這種對立關係真的很不舒服，

Content: 'cause you need them and they're usually really good friends of ours.
Content_zh: 因為你需要他們 他們通常是我們的好朋友

Content: I'm just getting mad at them one by one for breaking the faith of the backer agreement.
Content_zh: 我只是因為他們違背贊助者協議的信任，一個接一個地對他們生氣。

Content: It's annoying.
Content_zh: 很煩人

Content: Bailey: You know, one third of our, of all the articles that were written about Broken Age were about the embargo.
Content_zh: Bailey：你知道，所有關於《Broken Age》的文章裡，有三分之一是在講禁評期。

Content: And then another third were about us going from early access changing it to a regular release.
Content_zh: 另外三分之一是在講我們從 Early Access 改成一般發行。

Content: And then one third were about the game. Yeah. So.
Content_zh: 然後三分之一才是在講遊戲。對。所以……

Content: Rice: If we're saying backer Beta's the 14th and public release is the 28th, that would give the press builds like the 12th or something.
Content_zh: Rice：如果我們說 Backer Beta 是 14 號，公開發行是 28 號，那媒體版本大概會在 12 號之類的時候給。

Content: And embargo them, Bailey: This is an interesting one, because I think this is the next one that could lead to a PR blowup.
Content_zh: 然後設禁評期，Bailey：這件事很有意思，因為我覺得這是下一個可能引發公關災難的點。

Content: Bailey: Yeah. I was not in favor of the embargo.
Content_zh: Bailey：對。我原本就不贊成禁評期。

Content: Rice: But preorders are there like you said.
Content_zh: Rice：但就像你說的，預購已經開了。

Content: So people can still at least go and get the preorder.
Content_zh: 所以大家至少還是可以去預購。

Content: Bailey: So the preorders are, they're less than we expected.
Content_zh: Bailey：所以預購量，比我們預期的少。

Content: They are, we were hoping that they'd come in higher.
Content_zh: 是，我們本來希望它們會更高。

Content: If I had to project off the preorders we received so far, that would be a kind of bleak outlook as far as how many we'd actually sell,
Content_zh: 如果要根據目前收到的預購量來推估，從我們實際能賣多少來看，前景會有點黯淡，

Content: you know, for the lifetime of Broken Age.
Content_zh: 你知道，以《Broken Age》的整個銷售週期來說。

Content: But I'm basing everything off if different historicals for other games that Tim has sold and none of those have had this kind of Kickstarter story.
Content_zh: 但我的推估是根據 Tim 過去賣過的其他遊戲成績，而那些遊戲都沒有這種 Kickstarter 故事。

Content: So this is definitely unique.
Content_zh: 所以這絕對很特別。

Content: I don't think it's time for us to panic right now, but it puts a whole lot more emphasis and importance on January 28th
Content_zh: 我覺得現在還不到我們該恐慌的時候，但這讓 1 月 28 日的重要性大幅提高，

Content: when it comes out for real.
Content_zh: 也就是它真正上市的時候。

Content: 'Cause I think it will do well in the long-run, but it's like how long is that run.
Content_zh: 因為我覺得長期來看它會賣得不錯，但問題是那個長期到底有多長。

Content: The other piece that I think is kind of silver-lining in all this is the reviews are coming in really good.
Content_zh: 另一個我覺得算是這整件事裡一線希望的部分，是評價都非常好。

Content: You know, I foresee that the game's going to have a great word-of-mouth buzz.
Content_zh: 你知道，我預期這款遊戲會有很棒的口碑聲量。

Content: Most of Tim's games actually do.
Content_zh: Tim 大多數遊戲其實都是這樣。

Content: And they have a, it has a very long tail.
Content_zh: 而且它們，它的長尾效應很明顯。

Content: There're games, what people call "evergreen games", they keep on selling at a certain, certain level and they just keep on,
Content_zh: 有些遊戲，大家稱為「長青遊戲」，會一直維持在某個銷售水準，然後持續賣下去，

Content: which keeps on accumulating over the years, which is different than most games.
Content_zh: 多年下來會不斷累積，這跟大多數遊戲不同。

Content: Most games have that huge spike, that hump, that six-weeks hump.
Content_zh: 大多數遊戲都會有那種巨大高峰，那個隆起，那個六週的高峰。

Content: Tim's are different.
Content_zh: Tim 的遊戲不一樣。

Content: So that's one good thing to take into account with all the good reviews and all the social media and the good word of mouth we're having.
Content_zh: 所以把所有好評、社群媒體和我們擁有的好口碑都算進去，這是一件值得考量的好事。

Content: You know, it's like we can't get to a place where we could sell this game quick enough,
Content_zh: 你知道，我們就是沒辦法快點到達可以賣這款遊戲的時候，

Content: because that's where we're really going to be able to see and project, you know, what the success of the game's going to be.
Content_zh: 因為到那時我們才真正能看出並推估這款遊戲會有多成功。

Content: Rice: So the other thing Tim was talking about is just that now that we have to move the embargo up and reviews are out now,
Content_zh: Rice：所以 Tim 提到的另一件事，就是既然現在我們得把禁評期提前，評測也已經出來了，

Content: the idea of flipping the switch on Steam earlier.
Content_zh: 是不是要早點打開 Steam 上的開關。

Content: Schafer: Releasing it a, a week earlier.
Content_zh: Schafer：提早一、一週發行。

Content: Like next Tuesday.
Content_zh: 像下週二。

Content: Schafer: Sure, whatever.
Content_zh: Schafer：當然，隨便了

Content: and like, you know, Schafer: Well, that's the question.
Content_zh: 然後，像是，你知道，Schafer：嗯，問題就在這裡。

Content: I mean, and there's, I mean there's a legitimate business reason, I think about it just because it's out there,
Content_zh: 我是說，而且，我是說我覺得有正當的商業理由，因為它已經在外面了，

Content: it's getting a lot of attention right now and there's a lot of press I'm going to do in the next week.
Content_zh: 現在受到很多關注，而且下週我會做很多媒體訪問。

Content: And it'd be nice if people, I mean can go pre-purchase it, but they can't buy it.
Content_zh: 如果大家，我是說，可以去預購，但不能真正購買，那就有點可惜。

Content: Rice: It'd be nice to have a story about the game being pushed forward.
Content_zh: Rice：最好能講一下遊戲往前推進的狀況。

Content: Franzke: I voiced my concern about this plan.
Content_zh: Franzke：我有表達我對這個計畫的擔憂。

Content: I mean technically it does make like, like just looking at how the long the things take, just doesn't really make all that much sense.
Content_zh: 我是說，光從技術面來看，看看這些事情要花多久，其實就不太合理。

Content: Franzke: And you know, I explained like that.
Content_zh: Franzke：你知道，我這樣解釋的

Content: That we can certainly do it but it will be very unadvisable, especially considering that time when that was first proposed.
Content_zh: 我們當然做得到，但那會非常不明智，尤其是考慮到當初提出這件事的時間點。

Content: We still had big problems with crashes and people not being able to play the game at all.
Content_zh: 我們當時還有嚴重的當機問題，有些人甚至完全沒辦法玩遊戲。

Content: So that was my main worry, that you know, we were still like trying to find the really big issues and so.
Content_zh: 所以那是我主要擔心的事，我們當時還在找那些真正的大問題。

Content: So we didn't feel like it was ready to ship it then.
Content_zh: 所以我們覺得那時候還沒準備好推出。

Content: But I think now, basically I don't think anything will have changed if we final release it on the planned day we had initially,
Content_zh: 但我現在基本上覺得，就算照我們一開始規劃的日期正式發布，也不會有什麼不同，

Content: just because technically time-wise it doesn't really work out any other way, anyways.
Content_zh: 因為以技術層面的時程來說，反正也沒有其他做法。

Content: Schafer: But it's good.
Content_zh: Schafer：不過這樣很好。

Content: So now the official reviews, now they are rolling in.
Content_zh: 所以現在正式評論陸續出來了。

Content: The great thing is that they're all really great.
Content_zh: 最棒的是，每一篇都很棒。

Content: I mean, that Polygon one was nine out of ten, which is great.
Content_zh: 我是說，Polygon 那篇給了九分，滿分十分，這很棒。

Content: I mean, I would give it a ten, but I'll accept a nine.
Content_zh: 我是說，我會給十分，但九分我也可以接受。

Content: Polygon Review: Broken Age may be unfinished, but it's also delightful, beautiful, utterly charming
Content_zh: Polygon Review：《Broken Age》或許還沒完成，但它令人愉快、美麗，而且迷人至極

Content: and you really should play it right this second.
Content_zh: 你真的應該現在馬上去玩。

Content: Stapley: No, it's cool.
Content_zh: Stapley：不，這很酷。

Content: It's awesome that it sounded like, that we all made an awesome thing now.
Content_zh: 聽起來很棒，好像我們現在真的做出了一個很棒的東西。

Content: Like this is the cool part, you know.
Content_zh: 這就是很酷的地方，你懂吧。

Content: Like we made this awesome thing and now it's out in the world for everybody to look at.
Content_zh: 我們做出了這麼棒的東西，現在它已經在世界上讓大家看到了。

Content: Rice: Yeah, I guess. Stapley: OK. How many reviews are there now.
Content_zh: Rice：對，我想也是。Stapley：好。現在有幾篇評論了？

Content: Crook: But it feels good.
Content_zh: Crook：但感覺很好

Content: And people seem to be really responding to the game.
Content_zh: 而且大家似乎真的很有共鳴。

Content: The reviews have been really good.
Content_zh: 評論真的很好

Content: And even comments about animation, which you don't get that often.
Content_zh: 甚至還有人評論動畫，這其實不常見。

Content: It seems like in games a lot of times, maybe you will a couple of times.
Content_zh: 在遊戲裡好像很多時候，也許偶爾會有。

Content: But there's been several other reviews that have mentioned the animation, that they really, that they really liked it.
Content_zh: 但另外有好幾篇評論都提到動畫，說他們真的很喜歡。

Content: And that's, that's rewarding.
Content_zh: 這點很有成就感。

Content: Oh, yeah. Yeah.
Content_zh: 是的 是啊。

Content: I saw a nine today; that's nice.
Content_zh: 我今天看到一個九分，滿不錯的。

Content: But you know, I actually, I feel kind of bad I don't really, I try not to read a lot of the reviews
Content_zh: 不過你知道，其實我有點不好意思，我真的盡量不要看太多評論，

Content: because the bad ones do kind of make me, put me down.
Content_zh: 因為負評真的會讓我有點沮喪。

Content: You know, they kind of, you know, when you're kind of on this high that you've released it
Content_zh: 你知道，當你因為遊戲發布而情緒很高昂時，

Content: and then suddenly, even though if there's like six good reviews and one bad one, that bad one kind of affects me.
Content_zh: 突然就算有六篇好評、一篇負評，那篇負評還是會影響我。

Content: So I try not to read a lot of the comments until a little bit later on.
Content_zh: 所以我會盡量晚一點再去看很多留言。

Content: Heatseeker Review: Broken Age gets by on the strength of its storytelling.
Content_zh: Heatseeker Review：《Broken Age》靠著它的敘事魅力撐了起來。

Content: The puzzles aren't particularly complex or compelling with the exception of a certain vomiting tree.
Content_zh: 謎題並不特別複雜或吸引人，除了某棵會嘔吐的樹之外。

Content: And serve as a reason to explore the environments.
Content_zh: 它們比較像是讓玩家探索環境的理由。

Content: I can't say I recommend this to people who don't already have a fondness for this genre.
Content_zh: 我不能說我會推薦給本來就不喜歡這類型的人。

Content: Compared to modern entries in the genre like those from Telltale Games, it may seem a little simple or even boring.
Content_zh: 跟 Telltale Games 這類現代同類作品相比，它可能顯得有點簡單，甚至無聊。

Content: Schafer: This is actually just new posts, for new posts today.
Content_zh: Schafer：這其實只是新貼文，今天的新貼文。

Content: Oh God. The guy wants a refund.
Content_zh: 天啊 他想要退款

Content: It's like, ah. This is a jerk.
Content_zh: 就像，啊，這傢伙真討厭。

Content: I'm just going to say it.
Content_zh: 我就直說了。

Content: This is a jerk.
Content_zh: 真是混蛋

Content: Thanks for backing, jerk.
Content_zh: 謝謝你的贊助，混蛋。

Content: Franzke: You know, it's unrealistic to expect that people will be able to please everybody, obviously.
Content_zh: Franzke：你知道，期望人們能取悅所有人是不切實際的。

Content: Unfortunately very often the people who are the most dissatisfied are also the loudest ones, you know, which can be difficult,
Content_zh: 很可惜，最不滿的人往往也是最大聲的人，這可能會很難受，

Content: especially if you're pouring a lot of effort and a lot of work and a lot of your like, like "soul's" the wrong word,
Content_zh: 尤其是當你投入了大量心力、很多工作，還有很多你的……說「靈魂」不太對，

Content: but like a lot of your personal kind of effort into the game.
Content_zh: 但就是你很多個人的努力都放進遊戲裡時。

Content: Like to see somebody come in and like just completely smash it.
Content_zh: 看到有人跑進來，然後就把它徹底打爛。

Content: I did the mistake and I should know better after so many years in games.
Content_zh: 我犯了錯，在遊戲業這麼多年，我應該更清楚才對。

Content: I think I went in Kotaku where they had an article about the "Retro Mode".
Content_zh: 我想我是點進了 Kotaku，他們有一篇關於「復古模式」的文章。

Content: And like one of the comments, and you should never do that if you work in games.
Content_zh: 然後其中一則留言，你做遊戲的話真的絕對不該那樣。

Content: Never read the comments.
Content_zh: 絕對不要看留言。

Content: Actually, generally don't read any internet comments.
Content_zh: 其實，一般不看任何網路評論。

Content: I scroll down and somebody was like, this just look shit.
Content_zh: 我往下捲，看到有人說，這看起來根本爛透了。

Content: Like this is, you know, here, here's a comparison to a hand painted, hand painted sprites, versus down-res
Content_zh: 像是，這裡拿手繪、手繪 sprite 跟降解析度版本比較，

Content: It's like, yes, obviously.
Content_zh: 我就想，對啊，當然。

Content: And then backers immediately jumped in "Well they just put it in as a little add on" Still the person was not satisfied with that at all.
Content_zh: 然後贊助者立刻跳出來說：「他們只是把它當成小附加功能放進去。」但那個人還是完全不滿意。

Content: And then he's like, well, if you were a backer you would see that, you know, they just put in during the weekend as a little side project.
Content_zh: 接著他說，如果你是贊助者就會知道，他們只是週末把它當成小支線專案放進去。

Content: I'm not a backer but this is shit.
Content_zh: 我不是贊助者，但這就是爛。

Content: I'm not going to buy this.
Content_zh: 我不會買這個。

Content: It gets, [throws hands up, exasperated].
Content_zh: 就會變成，[沮喪地舉起雙手]。

Content: Having said all that, overall I think people are very happy.
Content_zh: 話雖如此，整體來說，我覺得大家都很開心。

Content: I've got a lot of Tweets from people who are super happy with the project.
Content_zh: 我收到很多推文，都是對這個專案超滿意的人。

Content: On the forums people are very happy, you know.
Content_zh: 在論壇上人們都很高興

Content: We saw someplace where some people like really, really enjoying the game.
Content_zh: 我們看到有些地方，有些人真的非常非常享受這款遊戲。

Content: And like, you know, it's good to see super-positive reactions of somebody just like laughing about all the jokes in the game.
Content_zh: 看到有人對遊戲裡所有笑話都笑得很開心，這種超正面的反應很棒。

Content: And just generally like finding the game very beautiful and stuff like that.
Content_zh: 整體來說也覺得遊戲很漂亮之類的。

Content: So it's good.
Content_zh: 所以這很好。

Content: Web Streamer: Fuck yeah!
Content_zh: Web Streamer：太爽了！

Content: Web Streamer: I don't know! Which one! What do I pick!
Content_zh: Web Streamer：我不知道！哪一個！我要選什麼？

Content: Oohh.
Content_zh: 喔。

Content: Shay: This must be where Marek sleeps.
Content_zh: Shay：這一定是 Marek 睡覺的地方。

Content: Web Streamer: So he keeps the costume on while he sleeps.
Content_zh: Web Streamer：所以他睡覺時也穿著那套服裝。

Content: That's funny.
Content_zh: 真有趣

Content: Man, this is a genius game, genius story.
Content_zh: 天啊，這遊戲太天才了，故事也太天才了。

Content: Schafer: I'm super happy that no one seems to have guessed the big spoiler at the end of it.
Content_zh: Schafer：我超開心，似乎沒有人猜到最後那個大劇透。

Content: I really thought that was going to get out.
Content_zh: 我真的以為那會被傳出去。

Content: And actually long ago on the forums, one guy guessed it.
Content_zh: 其實很久以前在論壇上 有個人猜到了

Content: And no one followed up on it.
Content_zh: 但沒有人接著討論。

Content: Like no one, he just said his little thing, hey, what if this is the case?
Content_zh: 就沒有人，他只是說了自己的小想法，嘿，如果是這樣呢？

Content: And no one answered him.
Content_zh: 沒人回答他

Content: I was like, oh man.
Content_zh: 我就想，天啊。

Content: I feel, oh God.
Content_zh: 我覺得，噢天啊。

Content: You know, I hate that guy for guessing it, but I'm really, it's worth something.
Content_zh: 你知道，我討厭那傢伙猜到了，但那真的很有價值。

Content: We should send him some chocolate.
Content_zh: 我們應該給他送點巧克力

Content: But everyone else has just had the exact like experience that I was hoping for, which is that they were really like, oh my God!
Content_zh: 但其他每個人都得到了我希望他們有的那種體驗，就是他們真的會說，天啊！

Content: I didn't see that coming, that's crazy.
Content_zh: 我沒想到會這樣 太瘋狂了

Content: Web Streamer: It's Shay!
Content_zh: Web Streamer：是 Shay！

Content: Shay was controlling Mog Chothra.
Content_zh: Shay 一直在控制 Mog Chothra。

Content: Web Streamer: Uhhh, Shay?
Content_zh: Web Streamer：呃，Shay？

Content: Web Streamer: What the heck?
Content_zh: Web Streamer：怎麼回事？

Content: What the heck?
Content_zh: 怎麼回事？

Content: That's Shay!
Content_zh: 那是 Shay！

Content: Schafer: But they all seemed to really be enjoying the ending.
Content_zh: Schafer：但他們似乎都很喜歡結局

Content: And so I'm really happy with that.
Content_zh: 所以我真的很高興

Content: And I'm really happy with people's response to like the story, the characters.
Content_zh: 而且我很開心大家對故事、角色的反應。

Content: And a lot of people, I think, will actually appreciate the difficulty level in the game once we get out of the most hardcore part of the audience.
Content_zh: 而且我覺得，等我們跳脫最核心硬派的那群觀眾後，很多人其實會欣賞遊戲的難度。

Content: I think they'll like that, that it flows more as opposed to hitting a series of really, really hard stops, for most people.
Content_zh: 我覺得大多數人會喜歡它更順暢，而不是碰上一連串非常非常硬的卡關點。

Content: Schafer: I mean, you can tell they already anticipated that.
Content_zh: Schafer：我是說，你看得出來他們其實早就預料到了。

Content: After play-testing the first part, I was like, I think this is a little easy, but I think rather than just throwing a monkey wrench, Monkey Island joke,
Content_zh: 試玩測試第一部分之後，我就想，這好像有點簡單，但與其硬塞一個《Monkey Island》式的難題笑話，

Content: in there, I'm really focusing on Act Two and making Act Two a little more, instead of things being one step, having them be a little more complex.
Content_zh: 我更專注在第二幕，讓第二幕多一點東西，不只是一步就解決，而是稍微複雜一點。

Content: So I'm way ahead of them, way ahead of them on that.
Content_zh: 所以這方面我早就走在他們前面了，早很多。

Content: Petty: You know, I think, yeah, it's interesting.
Content_zh: Petty：你知道，我覺得，對，這很有趣。

Content: Like the, I don't, I don't think I read anything that I felt was unfair and it all seemed pretty balanced.
Content_zh: 像是，我不覺得我讀到任何讓我覺得不公平的東西，而且整體看起來都滿平衡的。

Content: There were, I think, you know, there's some comments about length, maybe a couple of them.
Content_zh: 我想有一些人提到長度，也許有幾則。

Content: And that's kind of difficult, because you know, I mean, this was the same on Stacking.
Content_zh: 那有點難處理，因為你知道，我是說，《Stacking》也是一樣。

Content: You know, you can, you can burn through an adventure game and, and that can really shorten your play experience.
Content_zh: 你知道，你可以很快衝完一款冒險遊戲，而那真的會縮短你的遊玩體驗。

Content: But I don't know if that's the best way to evaluate that particular game.
Content_zh: 但我不知道這是不是評價那款遊戲的最佳方式。

Content: You know, it's not a game that has a mechanic that just like is endlessly playable.
Content_zh: 你知道，它不是那種有一套可以無止境重複玩的機制的遊戲。

Content: So it's a little, I don't know, you know, it's just, it's just something that happens with adventure games that, I've seen that come up.
Content_zh: 所以這有點，我不知道，這就是冒險遊戲會遇到的事，我也看過這種說法。

Content: I don't necessarily disagree with it, but I guess I don't know what to say about it, you know.
Content_zh: 我不一定不同意 但我想我不知道該怎麼說

Content: But I think, you know, in some other stuff is stuff that we discussed early on that I've seen like, you know, the lack of a verb coin or a verb page.
Content_zh: 但我想，有些其他東西是我們早期討論過的，像是缺少動詞圓盤或動詞頁面。

Content: And I think that's just a choice Tim, and I think the correct choice Tim made early on, you know.
Content_zh: 我覺得那只是 Tim 的選擇，而且我覺得 Tim 一開始做的選擇是正確的。

Content: I don't think there's probably people played Machinarium that also was like, this would be better if I could have a look and examine and verb link.
Content_zh: 我覺得大概不會有人玩了《Machinarium》之後也說，如果我能有「看」、「檢查」和動詞連結會更好。

Content: But I don't really feel like the experience needed it in any way and I don't really feel like Broken Age needs it in anyway.
Content_zh: 但我真的不覺得那種體驗需要它，也不覺得《Broken Age》需要它。

Content: And I don't really feel like that's where we puzzle depth, mechanical depth anyways.
Content_zh: 而且我也不覺得那就是我們該增加謎題深度、機制深度的地方。

Content: So you know, I mean, I wouldn't oppose it if someone really, really, like we had started the game and we really, really wanted to take that approach.
Content_zh: 所以你知道，我是說，如果有人真的很想，如果我們一開始就很想採取那種做法，我也不會反對。

Content: But I just like the idea of you know, I think Tim's initial vision for the game would be like, well, what if adventure games just kept going?
Content_zh: 但我就是喜歡那個想法，我覺得 Tim 對這款遊戲最初的願景像是：如果冒險遊戲就只是繼續往前走呢？

Content: And we just were keeping it working.
Content_zh: 我們就是讓它繼續運作。

Content: We wouldn't keep making the exact same thing.
Content_zh: 我們不會一直做同樣的事

Content: We'd try and mess with the formula and advance things and I think that's what we tried to do with visuals.
Content_zh: 我們會試著改變公式、推進一些東西，我覺得我們在視覺上就是這麼做的。

Content: We didn't want to make retro visuals, right.
Content_zh: 我們不想做復古視覺，對吧。

Content: We wanted to make visuals that were 2D and charming, but also took advantage of modern methodologies and technologies and resolutions.
Content_zh: 我們想做出 2D 又迷人的視覺，同時也運用現代的方法、技術和解析度。

Content: And I think the approach to the game play is consistent with that.
Content_zh: 我覺得遊戲玩法的方向也跟這點一致。

Content: And this is a choice.
Content_zh: 而這是一個選擇。

Content: You know, and some people will want that exact Monkey Island moment and some people are like, thank God, the puzzles aren't that confusing, you know.
Content_zh: 你知道，有些人會想要那種原汁原味的《Monkey Island》時刻，有些人則會說，謝天謝地，謎題沒那麼讓人困惑。

Content: It's hard, it's hard to get the right.
Content_zh: 要拿捏正確很難，真的很難。

Content: Petty: So what I would like to do is next week, since Bagel's out for two weeks and I wanted him to come out at this time so we could kick off Act Two.
Content_zh: Petty：所以我想做的是下週，因為 Bagel 要離開兩週，而我希望他這時候先出來，這樣我們就能啟動第二幕。

Content: So I would like to kind of, at the very least, we'll see who has time.
Content_zh: 所以我想至少先看看誰有時間。

Content: At the very least Bagel and Levi and myself to start concept jamming next week on early Act Two stuff.
Content_zh: 至少 Bagel、Levi 和我，下週要開始針對第二幕早期內容做概念發想。

Content: And Tim hasn't delivered the final design or dialogue yet, but I already know some of high level stuff
Content_zh: Tim 還沒交出最終設計或對話，但我已經知道一些高層次的內容，

Content: and then hopefully we can get him to kind of, I can kind of like mention all that and he can kind of correct me
Content_zh: 希望我們可以讓他來，我可以先提一下那些東西，然後他可以修正我，

Content: where he's changed things and just get us going.
Content_zh: 告訴我們他改了哪些地方，讓我們開始動起來。

Content: So there's definitely a new, at least one new area that has no presence at all in the current act that we have to design.
Content_zh: 所以至少確定有一個全新的區域，在目前這一幕裡完全不存在，是我們必須設計的。

Content: And we had some very early stuff on it from before, but we haven't thought about it in 18 months.
Content_zh: 我們很久以前有一些非常早期的東西，但已經 18 個月沒去想它了。

Content: So we'll probably start there.
Content_zh: 我們可能會從那裡開始

Content: Stapley: So are these the only new, this is like pretty much all we have to worry, really worry about, right?
Content_zh: Stapley：所以這些就是唯一的新東西，差不多就是我們真正需要擔心的全部，對吧？

Content: I mean, those only new things?
Content_zh: 我是說，只有這些新東西嗎？

Content: Petty: Well, this is the new stuff we know about for sure.
Content_zh: Petty：嗯，這是我們目前確定知道的新東西。

Content: So I thought we'd start there. OK.
Content_zh: 所以我想我們就從那裡開始。好。

Content: So the thing that Tim, like he's going to be here after lunch, we can him some more questions
Content_zh: 所以 Tim 午餐後會過來，我們可以再問他更多問題，

Content: but the thing that we know for sure is that we need a monster lands area.
Content_zh: 但我們確定知道的是，我們需要一個怪物降落區域。

Content: It's probably also a good chance for us to think about where we want to go with, you know, as we're developing him,
Content_zh: 這大概也是個好機會，讓我們思考在發展 Marek 時想把他帶往哪個方向，

Content: if we want to go anyplace different with Marek.
Content_zh: 看看我們想不想讓 Marek 走向不同的地方。

Content: Here's another concept that we can think about.
Content_zh: 這是我們能想到的另一個概念

Content: Is we talked about, OK, what Alex's is.
Content_zh: 我們談過，Alex 那個到底是什麼。

Content: And originally Tim kept saying, well, it's, oh, this'll be a copy of Mog Chothra to save time.
Content_zh: 一開始 Tim 一直說，嗯，這會是 Mog Chothra 的複製版，好節省時間。

Content: And I was saying, well, that's not really going to save us much time.
Content_zh: 我就說，嗯，那其實不會幫我們省太多時間。

Content: I mean, it'll save a week or so of production time, but...
Content_zh: 我是說，可能會省下一週左右的製作時間，但……

Content: Stapley: It's got to be like older version.
Content_zh: Stapley：它應該要像比較舊的版本。

Content: Petty: Right. Like it's been buried.
Content_zh: Petty：對。像是它被埋起來了。

Content: It's not going to look the same.
Content_zh: 它看起來不會一樣。

Content: I'd love to see it like a previous generation.
Content_zh: 我很想看到它像是前一代的版本。

Content: Because when we were starting to draw like, I don't know, maybe, like some of these older ones or whatever,
Content_zh: 因為我們開始畫的時候，我不知道，也許像是這些比較舊的版本之類的，

Content: like remember we had all those ideas like, oh, it's got diamond-shape eyes instead of hex eyes.
Content_zh: 記得我們有那些想法嗎？像是，喔，它的眼睛是菱形，不是六角形。

Content: Ryken: Yeah, just like changes with times.
Content_zh: Ryken：對，就像會隨著時代改變。

Content: 400 years, like, oh yeah, but then he came around to hexes again.
Content_zh: 四百年後，好像又流行回六角形了。

Content: And then it went away for another 100 years.
Content_zh: 然後又消失了一百年。

Content: Petty: We added two sides to our windows.
Content_zh: Petty：我們幫窗戶多加了兩條邊。

Content: Stapley: That's awesome. That's always the best.
Content_zh: Stapley：太棒了 這總是最好的。

Content: Like in Star Wars, you're like, well, that's R2D2, but it's a fucking red R2D2 and it's like different.
Content_zh: 就像《Star Wars》裡，你會說，嗯，那是 R2D2，但他媽的是紅色的 R2D2，而且就是不一樣。

Content: Awesome. I always love that.
Content_zh: 太棒了 我一直很喜歡

Content: Petty: Well plus it would be cool, too, like this one doesn't have, it's been buried for however long
Content_zh: Petty：而且這樣也會很酷，像是這個沒有，它已經被埋了不知道多久，

Content: so it doesn't have to look as purely organic, you know.
Content_zh: 所以它不必看起來像純有機的，你知道的。

Content: Like we're not, we're no longer trying to make people believe it's just a monster.
Content_zh: 像是我們不再試著讓大家相信它只是個怪物。

Content: So being able to see some combination of the mechanical and the monster...
Content_zh: 所以能看到機械和怪物的結合

Content: Ryken: Yeah, like the outer hull is just like ripped away and most of the parts.
Content_zh: Ryken：對，像外殼被撕開，大部分零件也露出來了。

Content: Stapley: But it'll still be the same like rig kind of set up, right?
Content_zh: Stapley：但 rig 的設定還是差不多，對吧？

Content: Petty: I mean, it could be whatever we want.
Content_zh: Petty：我是說，它可以變成任何我們想要的樣子。

Content: Bog Chothra.
Content_zh: Bog Chothra。

Content: Stapley: Bog Chothra?
Content_zh: Stapley：Bog Chothra？

Content: Bog, I like that one.
Content_zh: Bog，我喜歡這個。

Content: I like Bog.
Content_zh: 我喜歡 Bog。

Content: Ryken: Just Bob.
Content_zh: Ryken：就叫 Bob。

Content: Stapley: Bob Chothra? Awesome.
Content_zh: Stapley：Bob Chothra？太棒了。

Content: That would be fucking sweet.
Content_zh: 那一定超讚。

Content: Stapley: All right. Should we call Scott and get pumped?
Content_zh: Stapley：好吧，要不要打給 Scott，讓大家嗨起來？

Content: Stapley: Yeah! Look, whoo!
Content_zh: Stapley：耶！看啊，嗚！

Content: Hey, bro, get us pumped up!
Content_zh: 嘿，兄弟，讓我們嗨起來！

Content: Get us pumped up!
Content_zh: 讓我們嗨起來！

Content: We're all like, boo, drawing is stupid.
Content_zh: 我們都在那邊，噓，畫畫好蠢。

Content: He still in bed?
Content_zh: 他還在床上嗎？

Content: Campbell: Chilling on the couch.
Content_zh: Campbell：在沙發上放鬆。

Content: Stapley: Oh, man.
Content_zh: Stapley：天啊

Content: Campbell: I was just, I was just watching Triplets of Belleville.
Content_zh: Campbell：我剛剛在看《佳麗村三姊妹》。

Content: Stapley: Oh, sweet, dude.
Content_zh: Stapley：喔，讚啦，老兄。

Content: Campbell: What are you guys, what are you guys doing?
Content_zh: Campbell：你們在幹什麼？

Content: Stapley: We're doing an art jam, dude.
Content_zh: Stapley：我們在做美術 jam，老兄。

Content: We're doing an art jam and nobody's pumped up and like, like nobody fucking is as psyched as you.
Content_zh: 我們在做美術 jam，可是沒人嗨得起來，沒人像你那麼有熱情。

Content: You got to get us psyched.
Content_zh: 你得讓我們嗨起來。

Content: You know, when you and Peter are here, you guys are like, ooh, ooh, ooh!
Content_zh: 你知道，你和 Peter 在的時候，你們都會喔、喔、喔！

Content: So come on, bro, do it, go for it.
Content_zh: 所以來吧，兄弟，做吧，衝了。

Content: Ryken: We need some art cheerleading.
Content_zh: Ryken：我們需要一點美術啦啦隊。

Content: Campbell: Get psyched, man.
Content_zh: Campbell：嗨起來，老兄。

Content: What are you guys working on? Tell me about it.
Content_zh: 你們在做什麼？跟我說說。

Content: Stapley: All right, check it out. Rice: What are you doing?
Content_zh: Stapley：好，來看這個。Rice：你在幹嘛？

Content: Stapley: Monster lands. Uh oh, look who it is.
Content_zh: Stapley：怪物降落。哎呀，看看是誰。

Content: What, tall man. Petty: Scott!
Content_zh: 什麼，高個子。Petty：Scott！

Content: Stapley: Yeah, we're all getting pumped. OK, cool.
Content_zh: Stapley：對，我們都嗨起來了。好，酷。

Content: This is one monster, man.
Content_zh: 這是個怪物

Content: Schafer: Who are you talking to?
Content_zh: Schafer：你在跟誰說話？

Content: Hi! Scott Campbell!
Content_zh: 嗨！Scott Campbell！

Content: Hi! Everybody's at the art jam.
Content_zh: 嗨！大家都在美術 jam。

Content: Stapley: I'm excited to hear about what we're going to do, because, 'cause I know that we all like, this is like,
Content_zh: Stapley：我很期待聽聽我們接下來要做什麼，因為我知道大家都覺得，這就像，

Content: not really trial and error but I got pretty much trial and error.
Content_zh: 不算完全靠試誤，但我幾乎就是靠試誤。

Content: You get closer to the end, everything started working really smoothly.
Content_zh: 越接近尾聲，一切就開始運作得很順。

Content: So you know, it's going to be way better for sure, you know, 'cause we learned so much.
Content_zh: 所以肯定會好很多，因為我們學到很多。

Content: But I'm, I'm actually excited to see how we take all the things that we learned, trial and error-wise
Content_zh: 但我其實很期待看到，我們會怎麼運用試誤學到的那些東西，

Content: and try to get it, you know, see what happens with the next, how organized we are, what we learned.
Content_zh: 看看接下來會怎樣，我們會變得多有組織，學到了什麼。

Content: Schafer: OK.
Content_zh: Schafer：好

Content: Stapley: Those are the new things.
Content_zh: Stapley：那些就是新的東西。

Content: Schafer: Monster lands and the gate Stapley: And there's only the pollution inside like the kingdom.
Content_zh: Schafer：怪物降落，還有城門。Stapley：而且裡面只有王國那邊的污染。

Content: Schafer: Don't film his armpits.
Content_zh: Schafer：不要拍他的腋下。

Content: Stapley: Oh God, are they like, oh God!
Content_zh: Stapley：天啊，是不是，天啊！

Content: Fuck. It's horrible.
Content_zh: 操 太可怕了

Content: Hey, bro.
Content_zh: 嘿，兄弟。

Content: Schafer: Aren't you glad you're not here right now?
Content_zh: Schafer：你現在不在這裡，是不是很慶幸？

Content: It would be nice to get the main characters some different outfits, just to make the game feel... Petty: So would she put on a space suit?
Content_zh: 讓主角們換幾套不同的衣服應該不錯，讓遊戲感覺……Petty：所以她會穿太空裝嗎？

Content: Schafer: I see her with the radiation suit and I don't know necessarily if he, I can't think of, I haven't thought of a puzzle.
Content_zh: Schafer：我想像她穿著防輻射衣，但我不確定他是不是，我想不到，我還沒想到相關謎題。

Content: What?
Content_zh: 什麼？

Content: Stapley: He could be in a cake dress.
Content_zh: Stapley：他可能穿著蛋糕裙

Content: Petty: We should do a transgender thing where he starts dressing like a girl, 'cause he gets the maiden,
Content_zh: Petty：我們可以做個跨性別的橋段，讓他開始穿得像女生，因為他拿到那些少女、

Content: leftover maiden's outfits and she dresses like a boy.
Content_zh: 剩下的少女服裝，而她穿得像男生。

Content: Schafer: I mean, he could put a druid cloak or he could, but he could put on a preener outfit.
Content_zh: Schafer：我是說，他可以穿德魯伊斗篷，或者也可以穿 preener 的服裝。

Content: remember we have those artworks, you had him masquerade as a preener for a while.
Content_zh: 記得我們有那些圖嗎？你讓他假扮成 preener 一陣子。

Content: He could possibly have to do that.
Content_zh: 他或許可以那樣做。

Content: I just think it would be more fun for the player to have them look really different.
Content_zh: 我只是覺得，讓他們看起來真的很不一樣，對玩家會比較有趣。

Content: Petty: He needs like a traveling cloak or something.
Content_zh: Petty：他需要像旅行斗篷之類的東西。

Content: Piece it together. Yeah.
Content_zh: 把它拼湊起來。對。

Content: [Music] Franzke: Other than doing some concept artwork,
Content_zh: [音樂] Franzke：除了做一些概念美術之外，

Content: nobody has to get started working on the second act anyways, so a lot of us are still working on bug fixes for this release.
Content_zh: 反正沒有人需要開始做第二幕，所以我們很多人還在替這次發行修 bug。

Content: And it's, you know, it's incremental update to the backer release.
Content_zh: 而且這是給贊助者版本的增量更新。

Content: So you know, I'm really optimistic that this is all like minor fixes to be put in.
Content_zh: 我真的很樂觀 這就像一些小修正

Content: But we're pretty solid, solid release.
Content_zh: 但我們這次發行版算滿穩的。

Content: Peck : We are investigating an issue with the track pad on Linux for laptops with track pads.
Content_zh: Peck：我們正在調查 Linux 筆電觸控板的問題。

Content: There's a little issue there.
Content_zh: 有一點小問題

Content: A little oversensitive, you might say.
Content_zh: 可以說是有點太敏感。

Content: Franzke: You make a change and like, oh, it works on Windows and then you go into Linux.
Content_zh: Franzke：你改了一個東西，想說喔，在 Windows 上可以，然後你進 Linux。

Content: Aaah! Gosh, it's broken.
Content_zh: 啊！天啊，壞掉了。

Content: What the hell is going on?
Content_zh: 怎麼回事？

Content: Franzke: It was good to a have Beta release for sure, to kind of find a lot of issues and all the things we ran into,
Content_zh: Franzke：有 Beta 版真的很好，可以找出很多問題，還有我們遇到的各種狀況，

Content: we were not unprepared for, but we didn't expect.
Content_zh: 不是說我們毫無準備，但確實沒預料到。

Content: Like a lot of Window compatibility issue than we expected, for example.
Content_zh: 例如 Windows 相容性問題比我們預期的多很多。

Content: And you know, that's why I have those guys here, like we had a lot of issues with compatibility,
Content_zh: 所以這就是我讓他們在這裡的原因，因為我們遇到很多相容性問題，

Content: so we ordered a lot of graphics cards just to kind of, trying to reproduce some of the issues that users were running into.
Content_zh: 所以我們訂了很多顯示卡，試著重現使用者遇到的一些問題。

Content: Franzke: Oh, this is what compatibility is all about.
Content_zh: Franzke：喔，這就是相容性測試的真諦。

Content: Finding really, really old hardware and putting in your machine and see what happens.
Content_zh: 找到非常非常舊的硬體，裝進你的機器裡，看看會發生什麼事。

Content: Franzke: You know, where here in the studio we didn't really have any problems running the game
Content_zh: Franzke：你知道，我們在工作室裡跑遊戲其實沒什麼問題，

Content: and like our external QA partner didn't have any problem running the game,
Content_zh: 我們的外部 QA 夥伴跑遊戲也沒什麼問題，

Content: but then as soon as we, you know, we launched it on, you know, on the day for the backers, like immediately people came back,
Content_zh: 但我們一在贊助者發行日推出，大家馬上就回報，

Content: oh, the game is crashing or sending out a black screen and stuff like that.
Content_zh: 喔，遊戲會當掉，或是出現黑畫面之類的。

Content: That's always like, crap.
Content_zh: 那總是會讓人覺得，糟了。

Content: If we would not have done it like that, we would have eventually run into this issues so you know,
Content_zh: 如果我們沒有這樣做，遲早還是會遇到這些問題，

Content: on the 28th we would have released the game and people would have had big problems with it and like,
Content_zh: 到了 28 號我們正式發行遊戲時，玩家就會遇到大問題，

Content: while crashes are not great, but at least acceptable during a Beta,
Content_zh: 雖然當機不算好事，但在 Beta 期間至少還可以接受，

Content: like once the game is out there, you know, it becomes very less acceptable.
Content_zh: 可是一旦遊戲正式推出，就會變得很難接受。

Content: Franzke: This VGA out. Haven't seen that in a while.
Content_zh: Franzke：這是 VGA 輸出。好久沒看到這個了。

Content: I don't know if it's even still possible.
Content_zh: 我不知道現在還能不能用。

Content: Franzke: I had, I think two crashes on Windows 98.
Content_zh: Franzke：我記得在 Windows 98 上當了兩次。

Content: I mean, I was surprised that you managed to download Steam on that machine, I guess.
Content_zh: 我是說，你居然能在那台機器上下載 Steam，我滿驚訝的。

Content: But yeah, unfortunately the game does not run on Windows 98 and I don't think it's part of the minimum requirement.
Content_zh: 但很遺憾，遊戲不能在 Windows 98 上跑，而且我不覺得那是最低需求的一部分。

Content: But yeah, that was, that was certainly very funny and surprising for sure.
Content_zh: 不過對，那確實很好笑，也很令人意外。

Content: Franzke: I knock on the graphics card right here.
Content_zh: Franzke：我現在先敲敲這張顯示卡。

Content: Romero: And for right now we're in shooting crashes and blockers and people's progress is lost, saved and corrupted stuff like that.
Content_zh: Romero：目前我們在處理當機、阻擋進度的問題，還有玩家進度遺失、存檔毀損之類的狀況。

Content: I mean, for now it's like the forum is the bug list pretty much, 'cause there's so much coming in.
Content_zh: 我是說，現在論壇幾乎就是 bug 清單，因為湧進來的東西太多了。

Content: So before I was in, you know, I was looking through our bugs and now I'm looking through all these bugs.
Content_zh: 所以以前我是在看我們自己的 bug，現在則是在看這些全部的 bug。

Content: Now they're the testers.
Content_zh: 現在他們就是測試人員。

Content: They're reporting the bugs to us.
Content_zh: 他們在向我們報告 bug。

Content: We're merely the conduit through which, you know, we get the bugs to developer.
Content_zh: 我們只是通道，把 bug 轉給開發者。

Content: Lui: It's nice, I think, for the team to have an overview of how the overall progress is going.
Content_zh: Lui：我覺得讓團隊能掌握整體進度很不錯。

Content: So it's nice to be able to see, like look at the chart and be like, oh, we are constantly going down.
Content_zh: 能看著圖表說，喔，我們一直在往下降，感覺很好。

Content: And there are spikes, like this spike over there is when Tim played through the game twice and submitted about fifteen pages of bug fixes.
Content_zh: 也會有高峰，像那邊那個高峰，就是 Tim 把遊戲玩了兩遍，提交了大概十五頁 bug 修正的時候。

Content: Schafer: Should realize that it's gotta be done this week.
Content_zh: Schafer：要意識到這週就得完成。

Content: So if there's anything that I really care about getting fixed, I have to make a big stink about it right now.
Content_zh: 所以如果有什麼我真的很在意、想修好的東西，我現在就得大聲抱怨。

Content: But making big stinks is what I'm all about.
Content_zh: 而大聲抱怨正是我的專長。

Content: The camera's here and then when I click, ah, the camera moves.
Content_zh: 攝影機就在這裡，然後我點擊，啊，攝影機就動了。

Content: So annoying.
Content_zh: 真煩人

Content: Vella's first step makes camera twitch.
Content_zh: Vella 第一步讓鏡頭抽動。

Content: At the end of the game, there's just a million bugs with things flashing or twitching for one frame.
Content_zh: 到了遊戲後段，到處都是東西閃一下或抽動一格的 bug。

Content: Drives me nuts.
Content_zh: 我快瘋了

Content: When she gives him the cupcake, that's where the cupcake appears floating in midair for one frame.
Content_zh: 她把杯子蛋糕給他的時候，杯子蛋糕會有一格浮在半空中。

Content: Yep. The average player can't say what happened, but they'll just get this weird twitchy feeling
Content_zh: 對。一般玩家說不出發生了什麼事，但他們會有那種奇怪的抽動感，

Content: when they're playing the game of like, aaah, something was just, it's like, just a feeling of it not being polished.
Content_zh: 玩遊戲時會覺得，啊，好像有什麼，就是感覺不夠打磨。

Content: But I can see, like OK, that camera was wrong for one frame.
Content_zh: 但我看得出來，像是，好，那一格鏡頭錯了。

Content: Sometimes I can't even tell if I saw a flash.
Content_zh: 有時候我甚至都看不清有沒有看到閃光

Content: Like at the end of that cutscene, I swear I saw a flash.
Content_zh: 像那段過場動畫的結尾，我發誓我看到閃了一下。

Content: I'm trying to fix everything.
Content_zh: 我在努力修復一切

Content: You can't fix everything.
Content_zh: 你不能把一切都修好

Content: The mouse sensitivity is weird, though.
Content_zh: 不過滑鼠靈敏度很怪。

Content: Peck: Myself, I've been, I got a couple of small little crashes in the UI, stuff like that, that I've been fixing.
Content_zh: Peck：我自己最近在修 UI 裡幾個小小的當機之類的問題。

Content: And but yeah, mostly like, these are my bugs right now.
Content_zh: 不過對，現在大多就是這些是我的 bug。

Content: They all fit on one span of the window, so it's like, things are going well.
Content_zh: 它們都塞得進同一個視窗畫面，所以感覺事情進展得不錯。

Content: Schafer: How many bugs are left in Bugzilla?
Content_zh: Schafer：Bugzilla 裡還剩多少 bug？

Content: Is it really a sizeable amount still?
Content_zh: 真的還有很多嗎？

Content: Crook: Yeah. I mean these are all, they're all, they're, most of them are minor.
Content_zh: Crook：對。我是說這些都，大部分都只是小問題。

Content: Schafer: Who's making all these bugs?
Content_zh: Schafer：這些 bug 是誰做的？

Content: Crook: I mean they're just minor but we know the game's coming out again, you know, as the final.
Content_zh: Crook：我是說，它們只是小問題，但我們知道遊戲又要以正式版推出了。

Content: So, so we just want to make it as good as we can.
Content_zh: 所以我們想盡力而為

Content: We're going to be, most of us will roll off on Monday, onto other things for a while.
Content_zh: 我們大多數人週一就會暫時離開，去做別的事情。

Content: And come back.
Content_zh: 然後回來

Content: Schafer: OK, great.
Content_zh: Schafer：好極了

Content: Crook: We're going to have to assess at the end of the month, we got to figure out how much work is ready to just start doing.
Content_zh: Crook：我們得在月底評估，弄清楚有多少工作已經準備好可以開始做。

Content: Crook: Sorry. So yeah.
Content_zh: Crook：抱歉。所以對。

Content: Which, you know, some of the script is written but there's still more to go.
Content_zh: 你知道，有些劇本已經寫好了，但還有更多要做。

Content: So I'm not sure how much work there will be, just for the first, you know, couple weeks.
Content_zh: 所以我不確定前幾週會有多少工作。

Content: So we got to keep people busy, though.
Content_zh: 不過我們還是得讓大家有事做。

Content: We had three of our animators actually go off the project now, so they've moved onto other projects.
Content_zh: 我們有三位動畫師現在真的離開這個專案了，他們轉去做其他專案。

Content: Roberts: I'm leaving Broken Age.
Content_zh: Roberts：我要離開《Broken Age》了

Content: Because I'm moving onto another unannounced project.
Content_zh: 因為我要轉去另一個尚未公布的專案。

Content: And they'll be, the rest of the Broken Age team that's left on that project, they're going to continue on Broken Age Part Two.
Content_zh: 剩下還留在《Broken Age》專案上的團隊成員，會繼續做《Broken Age》第二幕。

Content: So yeah, the nature of this new project is such that I don't see myself going over and helping out on Act Two at all.
Content_zh: 所以對，這個新專案的性質讓我覺得，我完全不會過去幫第二幕。

Content: But you know, that's famous last words, so it could happen.
Content_zh: 但你知道，話說太滿總會出事，所以也不是不可能。

Content: I would love to, at least animate something, but we are getting pretty busy around here with other things
Content_zh: 我很想至少做點動畫，但我們這邊因為其他事情變得滿忙的，

Content: so yeah, that, that I can't see happening.
Content_zh: 所以對，我看不太可能發生。

Content: Offscreen Voice: Was it nice while it lasted?
Content_zh: 畫外音：這段時間還愉快嗎？

Content: Roberts: It was, yeah, yeah.
Content_zh: Roberts：是，是，是。

Content: It was a nice, I wasn't really expecting to work on this project at all, so it was kind of a nice surprise.
Content_zh: 很不錯，我本來完全沒想到會參與這個專案，所以算是個很棒的驚喜。

Content: And it was something that I wanted to work on.
Content_zh: 而且這是我想參與的東西。

Content: Of course, you know, everyone here wanted to, well, most people here, I can't say everyone,
Content_zh: 當然，你知道，這裡每個人都想，嗯，這裡大多數人，我不能說每個人，

Content: but most people here wanted to, you know, at least put their hands on this because,
Content_zh: 但這裡大多數人都想至少參與一下，因為，

Content: just because it made history for you know, not to sound too grandiose about it, but that's what it did, right?
Content_zh: 因為它創造了歷史，你知道，不是想講得太誇張，但它確實如此，對吧？

Content: I mean, there is history that was made here.
Content_zh: 我是說，這裡確實創造了歷史。

Content: And so everyone that saw that, you know, thinks wow, yeah, that's something I at least want to put something in there.
Content_zh: 所以每個看到這件事的人都會想，哇，對，那是我至少也想投入一點的東西。

Content: So OK. Goodbye, Broken Age.
Content_zh: 好吧。再見，《Broken Age》。

Content: Goodbye 2HB. Goodbye, Maya.
Content_zh: 再見，2HB。再見，Maya。

Content: Goodbye Double Fine.
Content_zh: 再見，Double Fine。

Content: Schafer: So we'll launch the game tomorrow and hopefully if we're not all here fixing bugs, we'll get to go out and have another,
Content_zh: Schafer：所以我們明天要發行遊戲，希望如果我們不用全都待在這裡修 bug，就能出去再喝一輪，

Content: we'll go back to Red's, I hope.
Content_zh: 我希望我們能回 Red’s。

Content: If it's still standing.
Content_zh: 如果它還在的話。

Content: And take the team out for drinks.
Content_zh: 帶團隊出去喝酒

Content: And before they all roll off, temporarily, onto other projects, I'd like to have them all hang out.
Content_zh: 在他們暫時離開去做其他專案之前，我想讓大家一起聚一下。

Content: Go have a drink and celebrate.
Content_zh: 去喝一杯慶祝吧

Content: 'Cause it's weird, it's like, you wish these launches were super-clean, like everyone could just say, everything was great!
Content_zh: 因為這感覺很怪，你會希望這些發行都超乾淨，大家都能直接說，一切都很棒！

Content: So hopefully, I mean, time usually just washes away all the smaller things that happened,
Content_zh: 所以希望，我是說，時間通常會沖淡發生過的那些小事，

Content: like that embargo thing and all that other stuff will be forgotten.
Content_zh: 像那個禁評期事件，還有其他那些事都會被忘掉。

Content: And people will just remember how we didn't have mouse sensitivity when we launched and now we do.
Content_zh: 大家只會記得我們發行時沒有滑鼠靈敏度，現在有了。

Content: No, that won't be the thing. Maybe.
Content_zh: 不，那不會是重點。也許吧。

Content: Rice: It's Elijah Wood's birthday today. Bailey: Oh wow.
Content_zh: Rice：今天是 Elijah Wood 的生日。Bailey：喔，哇。

Content: Rice: So this is a good birthday present, right? Bailey: Yeah.
Content_zh: Rice：所以這是很棒的生日禮物，對吧？Bailey：對。

Content: Rice: Tim, call Elijah and wish and wish him a happy birthday.
Content_zh: Rice：Tim，打給 Elijah，祝他生日快樂。

Content: Schafer: Call him? Rice: Yeah, like you do with your mom.
Content_zh: Schafer：打給他？ Rice：是啊，就像你和你媽媽一樣。

Content: Schafer: Hey, Elijah! Happy birthday. Oh, shoot.
Content_zh: Schafer：嘿，Elijah！生日快樂。喔，糟了。

Content: [Phone rings] Bailey: Yeah. It's interesting, though.
Content_zh: [電話鈴聲] Bailey：對。不過這很有趣。

Content: You think about Tim, like he's been working on this game for two years.
Content_zh: 想想 Tim，他已經做這款遊戲兩年了。

Content: It's now being commercially released but so much goes into, you know, the lead up to this.
Content_zh: 現在終於正式上市了，但在這之前其實投入了很多。

Content: And I guess I could understand.
Content_zh: 我想我能理解

Content: It's a little anti-climactic when you sit here and you just kind of like refresh the webpage and look at numbers.
Content_zh: 你坐在這裡，只是不停重新整理網頁、看數字，感覺有點不夠戲劇性。

Content: The interesting thing about price, though, is you know, we can charge 29 bucks and because I used to actually work with, you know,
Content_zh: 不過價格有趣的地方在於，我們可以賣 29 美元，而且因為我以前其實待過，

Content: at a publisher who did, you know, significant retail presence, I know the PNLs and like, literally our profit margin
Content_zh: 待過一家零售通路很大的發行商，我知道損益表，所以我們的利潤率，

Content: and the profit margin we have for like a $59 box product, are the same thing, because with the box product you got to like,
Content_zh: 跟一款 59 美元盒裝產品的利潤率其實是一樣的，因為盒裝產品要，

Content: you got preprint the stuff.
Content_zh: 要預先印製那些東西。

Content: You got all the materials, you got to ship it and you got to store it, you know.
Content_zh: 你要準備所有物料，要運送，還要倉儲。

Content: The extra store will take their cut.
Content_zh: 零售商也會抽成。

Content: There will be price protection involved.
Content_zh: 還會牽涉到價格保障。

Content: There's a huge amount of expense that goes into it.
Content_zh: 花費很大

Content: That like, yeah.
Content_zh: 就像，是的。

Content: Profit margins are equal.
Content_zh: 利潤率是相等的。

Content: So we just got a lot, we got rid of a lot of waste.
Content_zh: 所以我們只是省掉了很多、很多浪費。

Content: Oh, there it is.
Content_zh: 找到了

Content: Rice: All right, Justin. We're live.
Content_zh: Rice：好，Justin。我們上線了。

Content: Bailey: I see it.
Content_zh: Bailey：我看到了

Content: Rice: I'm going to push the update.
Content_zh: Rice：我要推送更新了。

Content: Bailey: Good. Do it.
Content_zh: Bailey：很好 快

Content: Bailey: And it's interesting, 'cause they'd like, when you get in a top position, it's probably at least a hundred, a hundred thousand,
Content_zh: Bailey：而且很有趣，因為如果登上榜首，那大概至少有十萬、十萬，

Content: a hundred fifty thousand dollars.
Content_zh: 十五萬美元。

Content: But it actually could be way north of that and I'd have no idea, because you're number one, right?
Content_zh: 但實際上可能遠高於那個數字，而我也不會知道，因為你是第一名，對吧？

Content: So you could be very, very high up.
Content_zh: 所以可能會高非常、非常多。

Content: But that would be, that would be an amazing day for us, we could, we get in that top position.
Content_zh: 如果我們能登上第一，那對我們來說會是超棒的一天。

Content: Schafer: Sitting at my desk, looking at a picture of me sitting at my desk.
Content_zh: Schafer：坐在我的桌前，看著我坐在桌前的照片。

Content: [Hums to self] Bailey: It's about an hour and twenty minutes delayed.
Content_zh: [哼歌] Bailey：大概延遲一小時二十分鐘。

Content: Bailey: Yeah. That will be when we actually see something.
Content_zh: Bailey：對。到時候我們才真的會看到一些東西。

Content: I'll know at 11:00, 11:30 I'll have like the first indication.
Content_zh: 11 點，11 點半，我應該就會有第一個跡象。

Content: I think the hour of celebration or reckoning or both has come.
Content_zh: 我想慶祝、算帳，或兩者兼具的時刻到了。

Content: Schafer: No, it's not Raz.
Content_zh: Schafer：不，不是 Raz。

Content: I don't see any boobs or penises on anything.
Content_zh: 我沒看到任何胸部或陰莖之類的東西。

Content: Choy: You have to look more closely.
Content_zh: Choy：你得仔細看看

Content: Are you guys doing a standup?
Content_zh: 你們要開站會嗎？

Content: Schafer: We are going to do standups. Standups? Rice: Yeah.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們要開站會。站會？Rice：對。

Content: Schafer: Whoever's left, whoever's left on the team, come on to standups.
Content_zh: Schafer：團隊裡還在的人，剩下的人，都過來開站會。

Content: We're now live for everybody in the world to buy the game on Steam.
Content_zh: 現在全世界的人都可以在 Steam 上買這款遊戲了。

Content: That's it. That's all I had to say today.
Content_zh: 好了 這就是我今天要說的

Content: OK. So Part Two.
Content_zh: 好 第二部分

Content: It's like a back massage.
Content_zh: 就像背部按摩

Content: Bailey: Oh yeah, it's pretty good. That's not bad.
Content_zh: Bailey：喔對，還不錯。這樣不錯。

Content: It's not, it's not as good now.
Content_zh: 現在沒那麼好了。

Content: Schafer: It feels nice, yeah.
Content_zh: Schafer：感覺不錯

Content: Schafer: So if the sales aren't good, I'll immediately smash your head with this so that you won't ever feel the pain of seeing bad sales.
Content_zh: Schafer：所以如果銷量不好，我會立刻用這個砸爛你的頭，這樣你就不用承受看到銷量慘淡的痛苦了。

Content: Bailey: That's good. Deal.
Content_zh: Bailey：很好 好

Content: All right, we doing this?
Content_zh: 好，我們要看了嗎？

Content: Let's do, let's first just look at the, let's see, Top Ten List.
Content_zh: 我們先看看前十名

Content: Schafer: I'm not going to look.
Content_zh: Schafer：我不會看的

Content: Bailey: Not looking at Top Ten List?
Content_zh: Bailey：不看前十名榜單？

Content: Schafer: I don't care.
Content_zh: Schafer：我不在乎

Content: I'm not going to look.
Content_zh: 我不會看的

Content: Oh, oh! Look at that! Schafer: Holy cow!
Content_zh: 喔，喔！看那個！Schafer：天啊！

Content: Bailey: You're number two on new releases.
Content_zh: Bailey：你在新品上市榜第二名。

Content: That has no component to sales.
Content_zh: 那跟銷量沒有關係。

Content: But top sellers, we are number four.
Content_zh: 但在暢銷榜上，我們第四名。

Content: All right. Whoa!
Content_zh: 好吧 哇！

Content: Oh, this is refreshed, the new releases.
Content_zh: 喔，新品上市榜重新整理了。

Content: Still number four. OK, cool. Sweet!
Content_zh: 還是第四名。好，酷。太棒了！

Content: Fifty thousand, five ninety-nine.
Content_zh: 五萬零五百九十九。

Content: That is 2,000 units on Steam. Schafer: Hm?
Content_zh: 那是在 Steam 上賣了兩千套。Schafer：嗯？

Content: Bailey: That's 2,000 units on Steam.
Content_zh: Bailey：那是在 Steam 上賣了兩千套。

Content: Yeah, this is good.
Content_zh: 很好

Content: This will, this will be good.
Content_zh: 這會很好

Content: If we get over 100K today, I think that's a, it's a very good day and I expect that European sales usually hit overnight.
Content_zh: 如果我們今天超過十萬，我覺得那就是很棒的一天，而且歐洲銷量通常會在半夜進來。

Content: So I would expect that to double.
Content_zh: 所以我希望這能加倍

Content: Schafer: Double today. Bailey: Yeah.
Content_zh: Schafer：今天翻倍。Bailey：對。

Content: Schafer: By the end of the day.
Content_zh: Schafer：今天結束前。

Content: Bailey: Between now and the end of the day.
Content_zh: Bailey：從現在到今天結束前。

Content: Probably more than double.
Content_zh: 可能不止兩倍

Content: This'll probably be like, I would say like 125 to 150, which means that would take us over nine million for Double Fine lifetime revenue.
Content_zh: 這大概會到，我會說 12.5 萬到 15 萬，這表示 Double Fine 的累計營收會突破九百萬。

Content: Schafer: Hm. Bailey: It should be very, very cool.
Content_zh: Schafer：嗯 應該很酷

Content: And I actually expect, I do expect the trend of the game to, to stay, we'll have a spike right now,
Content_zh: 而且我其實預期，我確實覺得這款遊戲的趨勢會維持，現在會有一波高峰，

Content: but I think it'll actually be pretty steady after that, so that's good.
Content_zh: 但我覺得之後會很穩定 所以很好

Content: Schafer: OK. Now pretend the cameras are off now. Bailey: Now pretend?
Content_zh: Schafer：好。現在假裝攝影機已經關了。Bailey：現在假裝？

Content: Schafer: Tell me what you really think.
Content_zh: Schafer：告訴我你真正的想法

Content: Bailey: It's, it is what I expected.
Content_zh: Bailey：這是我預期中的狀況。

Content: Schafer: OK. All right. Exciting. Cool.
Content_zh: Schafer：好。好吧。很刺激。酷。

Content: Now I don't want anyone to look at it.
Content_zh: 現在我不想讓任何人看

Content: I guess I'll go deisgn Act Two. Bailey: Yeah.
Content_zh: 我想我去設計第二幕好了。Bailey：對。

Content: Offscreen Voice: It's not earth shattering.
Content_zh: 畫外音：這不是什麼驚天動地的數字。

Content: Bailey: It's not earth shattering, but it's on track to do what we need it to do, you know, to get the game finished this year.
Content_zh: Bailey：不是驚天動地，但它正朝著我們需要的方向前進，可以讓我們今年把遊戲完成。

Content: The only unfortunate part about this whole thing is that I was looking at all of our news broadcasts
Content_zh: 這整件事唯一可惜的是，我在看我們所有的新聞曝光，

Content: and basically, you know, basic, all the news, all the exposure went out when the backers got it, like two weeks ago.
Content_zh: 基本上，所有新聞、所有曝光都在贊助者拿到遊戲時出去了，也就是大概兩週前。

Content: And people didn't have a chance to literally like, you know, hey, I'll, I'll buy it now and I can play it now.
Content_zh: 大家當時沒辦法真的說，嘿，我現在買，現在就能玩。

Content: You know, that's hard to, as like a, you know, value proposition for consumer, it's hard to see everybody talking about this and be like,
Content_zh: 你知道，從消費者的價值主張來看，看到大家都在討論這個，卻發現，

Content: oh, I can pay for it and still not get to play it.
Content_zh: 喔，我可以付錢，但還是玩不到。

Content: But it'll hopefully come out in the wash.
Content_zh: 不過希望最後會自己平衡掉。

Content: Rice: It was the last, I think it was very last bar.
Content_zh: Rice：是在最後一個，我想是最後一根柱狀圖。

Content: Schafer: I think we can confirm now that we chose the right code name.
Content_zh: Schafer：我想我們現在可以確認 我們選擇了正確的代號

Content: It didn't sound like the right code name, but now look at it; Red's.
Content_zh: 當初聽起來不像正確的代號，但現在看看，Red’s。

Content: [Music] Schafer: Yeah. Let's talk about that.
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：對。我們來談談這件事。

Content: We shipped Red's last week.
Content_zh: 我們上週推出了 Red’s。

Content: I don't know if you guys noticed that.
Content_zh: 我不知道你們有沒有注意到這個

Content: We shipped the game, it was awesome.
Content_zh: 我們推出了遊戲，超棒的。

Content: Yah, Red's.
Content_zh: 對，Red’s。

Content: [Applause].
Content_zh: [掌聲]

Content: It has, everybody claps just for shipping it.
Content_zh: 你看，大家只是因為推出了就鼓掌。

Content: I think I've gotten claps out of you guys for like...
Content_zh: 我想我已經讓你們為這種事鼓掌過……

Content: Schafer: Yeah, yeah, yeah.
Content_zh: Schafer：是啊，是啊，是啊。

Content: And next week we're shipping Red's.
Content_zh: 下週我們要推出 Red’s。

Content: Next week it'll mean two weeks since we shipped Red's.
Content_zh: 下週就代表 Red’s 推出滿兩週。

Content: It has, it has made about $800,000 so far on the old Steam thing, which is fun.
Content_zh: 到目前為止，它在老 Steam 上大概賺了 80 萬美元，挺有趣的。

Content: And so that is an interesting number, because it was kind of a relief in one hand because we were like,
Content_zh: 所以那是個有趣的數字，因為某方面來說算是鬆了一口氣，因為我們本來想，

Content: get a lot of possibilities here, one of them is that we don't make any money.
Content_zh: 這裡有很多可能性，其中一個是我們一毛錢都賺不到。

Content: No one buys the game, we can't make Part Two.
Content_zh: 沒有人買這款遊戲，我們就做不了第二幕。

Content: But I think we, luckily, because that would have been an easy update, though.
Content_zh: 但我覺得我們很幸運，雖然那樣更新起來倒是很簡單。

Content: We would have called all the haters on Twitter and just been like, you guys were totally right.
Content_zh: 我們就會去 Twitter 上找所有酸民，然後說，你們完全說對了。

Content: I'm a huge a-hole and we're not going to do Part Two.
Content_zh: 我是個超級混蛋，我們不做第二幕了。

Content: But that doesn't seem to be a risk anymore.
Content_zh: 但那看起來已經不再是風險了。

Content: We are also kind of hoping like, what if it made so much money that we didn't ever have to make games anymore?
Content_zh: 如果它賺了那麼多錢 我們就不用再做遊戲了

Content: We could just hang around and play games all day in the office.
Content_zh: 我們可以整天待在辦公室玩遊戲

Content: Schafer: And we also don't make Act Two.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們也不做第二幕

Content: Yeah, either way Act Two is like.
Content_zh: 對，不管怎樣第二幕就是……

Content: Yeah, I mean, that's not a big enough number that we're going to be immediately, completely independent of publishers this year,
Content_zh: 對，我是說，這個數字還沒大到讓我們今年馬上完全不需要發行商，

Content: like it was a secret back in my head, kind of hope.
Content_zh: 雖然我腦中暗暗有這種希望。

Content: Where all my hopes are in the back of my head.
Content_zh: 我所有的希望都藏在腦子後面。

Content: Any other questions?
Content_zh: 還有其他問題嗎？

Content: Schafer: It's just under 20,000, I think. So, so far.
Content_zh: Schafer：大概不到兩萬吧 目前為止

Content: Schafer: We are. Yeah, so that's just to finish the whole Red's update.
Content_zh: Schafer：是的。對，所以那只是為了完成整個 Red’s 更新。

Content: What the team is working on now is a lot of, instead of diving into Part Two, is actually platform work.
Content_zh: 團隊現在在做的很多事，不是直接投入第二幕，而其實是平台相關工作。

Content: You get it out on more platforms to sell it on like IOS and the other platforms.
Content_zh: 要把它推到更多平台上銷售，像 iOS 和其他平台。

Content: Schafer: Sooner than we had previously thought we were going to do it.
Content_zh: Schafer：比我們之前想的要快

Content: Skillman: So it would be Act One available on iOS.
Content_zh: Skillman：所以會是在 iOS 上推出第一幕。

Content: Schafer: Yeah. OK. Let's talk about PR.
Content_zh: Schafer：是啊。 好 我們談談公關吧

Content: PR. What happened in the world of PR last week?
Content_zh: 公關 上週在公關世界發生了什麼事？

Content: Rice: Well, like you said we launched Broken Age.
Content_zh: Rice：就像你說的，我們推出了《Broken Age》。

Content: So it's been a lot of, yay! Yay!
Content_zh: 所以最近很多都是，耶！耶！

Content: [Applause].
Content_zh: [掌聲]

Content: Yeah. So it's been trying to keep that conversation going in the public as much as possible.
Content_zh: 對。所以一直在盡量讓大眾持續討論這件事。

Content: So those are ongoing kind of conversations.
Content_zh: 所以這些是正在進行的對話

Content: But yeah, now we're kind of shifting focus to AF stuff and getting everything ready to announce and launch that thing.
Content_zh: 不過對，現在我們有點把焦點轉到 AF 的事情上，準備好要宣布並推出那個東西。

Content: Schafer: Yeah, OK, that's it.
Content_zh: Schafer：是啊，好吧，就是這樣。

Content: I think all of you would agree with me that Chris Remo's a big jerk.
Content_zh: 我想你們都會同意我的看法，Chris Remo 是個大混蛋。

Content: I think, yeah.
Content_zh: 我想是的

Content: [Scattered applause] And furthermore we were all talking about it and decided we didn't like him.
Content_zh: [零星掌聲] 而且我們大家都討論過了，決定不喜歡他。

Content: So I don't want that to influence you at all, but I think we need to have, Chris Remo is moving onto hang out with his friends,
Content_zh: 所以我完全不想影響你們，但我想我們需要，Chris Remo 要離開去跟他的朋友混了，

Content: which is bad news for all of his, whatever you guys are.
Content_zh: 這對他的所有……不管你們算什麼人來說，都是壞消息。

Content: But, I've been saving that one all week.
Content_zh: 但我已經留了整整一週了

Content: So, but you're still here right now so, what happened last week in the world of the community of Double Fine?
Content_zh: 不過你現在還在這裡，所以，上週 Double Fine 社群界發生了什麼事？

Content: Remo: An important thing for our community is that we launched Broken Age [applause].
Content_zh: Remo：對我們社群來說，一件重要的事是我們推出了《Broken Age》。[掌聲]

Content: That actually has been a cool thing to see because I'm seeing a lot of stuff from people who have never even necessarily played adventure games before.
Content_zh: 這其實是件很酷的事，因為我看到很多從來不一定玩過冒險遊戲的人在討論。

Content: Schafer: I love all those people.
Content_zh: Schafer：我愛那些人

Content: Remo: Yeah. I mean there's, it's actually been kind of awesome to see, because a lot of them, you know, like with adventure game fans,
Content_zh: Remo：對。我的意思是，這其實很棒，因為很多人，你知道，像冒險遊戲粉絲，

Content: people come to, people come to it with like preconceptions and like all the, you know, the history that they have with the genre.
Content_zh: 大家接觸它時會帶著先入為主的想法，還有他們對這個類型累積的所有歷史。

Content: And that's cool because that's the people that the game was inspired by in the first place.
Content_zh: 這很酷，因為這些人本來就是這款遊戲最初的靈感來源。

Content: But it's also really cool to see all the reactions from people who don't have those preconceptions, because they'll be like, yeah,
Content_zh: 但看到那些沒有這些先入為主想法的人的反應，也真的很酷，因為他們會說，

Content: so I was playing this thing and like I didn't know what it was and it seemed pretty cool.
Content_zh: 我在玩這個東西，然後我不知道它是什麼，但感覺很酷。

Content: You just kind of walk around and like cool stuff happens.
Content_zh: 你就到處走走，然後有很酷的事情發生。

Content: And like it was actually really awesome.
Content_zh: 好像真的很棒

Content: And that kind of thing is surprisingly cool to read.
Content_zh: 這種東西讀起來意外地很酷。

Content: So I've been trying to keep up with that stuff, just around the web and it's been really inspiring and awesome.
Content_zh: 所以我一直在努力追這些網路上的內容，真的很鼓舞人心，也很棒。

Content: Schafer: Anything else?
Content_zh: Schafer：還有嗎？

Content: Mr. Remo? Anything? Anything else.
Content_zh: Remo 先生？還有嗎？還有別的嗎？

Content: Remo: I will miss you guys a lot.
Content_zh: Remo：我會想念你們的

Content: I really loved working here a lot and I'm bummed to be leaving.
Content_zh: 我真的很喜歡在這裡工作，也很難過要離開。

Content: Schafer: Well, we'll miss you, wish you well and we still think you're a big jerk.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們會想你的 祝你好運 我們還覺得你是個大混蛋

Content: Who agrees with me?
Content_zh: 誰同意我的看法？

Content: [Applause] [Music]
Content_zh: [掌聲] [音樂]

Content: Offscreen Voice: [singing] I guess he's not a monster, but he's so far away.
Content_zh: 畫外音：[唱歌] 我想他不是怪物，但他離得好遠。

Content: Shay, we're meant to be for longer than forever.
Content_zh: Shay，我們注定要比永遠更久。

Content: Offscreen Voice: Who cares about Shay?
Content_zh: 畫外音：誰在乎 Shay？

Content: I have evil deeds to do.
Content_zh: 我還有壞事要做

Content: I used to be good like everyone else and Shay, but no!
Content_zh: 我以前跟其他人還有 Shay 一樣善良，但現在不！

Content: I am actually evil.
Content_zh: 其實我是邪惡的

Content: Offscreen Voice: [singing] for longer than forever.
Content_zh: 畫外音：[唱歌] 比永遠更久。

Content: As constant as the stars.
Content_zh: 恆如恆星。

Content: I close my eyes and I am where you are.
Content_zh: 我閉上眼睛，就在你所在的地方。
